title: Progress Test
objectives: Assess sports and technology vocabulary from Unit 6
Test reading comprehension about a Science Museum visit
Evaluate grammar: good at + verb-ing and relative clauses (who/which)
Write a structured paragraph about Sports Day

exercises.problem: Riding bikes is a great exercise to ............ calories. (a. burn, b. worry, c. greet, d. wrap)
............, schools are modern in Kuwait. (a. Nowadays, b. Suddenly, c. Easily, d. Gently)
Modern cars have ............ to help us drive safely. (a. calories, b. channels, c. workouts, d. sensors)
Playing sports helps to ............ your energy. (a. announce, b. track, c. increase, d. land)
Read about Sara and Nouf visiting Science Museum. Best title: (a. The Science Museum, b. The Space Hall, c. The Robots, d. The Moon Landing)
The underlined word 'effortless' in line 10 means: (a. new, b. light, c. clean, d. easy)
The underlined word 'They' in line 9 refers to: (a. photos, b. tablets, c. girls, d. cars)
Noura is not good at ............ bikes. (a. rode, b. riding, c. rides)
Mona ............ has a long hair, is my cousin. (a. which, b. who, c. what)
I ............ good at using smart phones. (a. is, b. am, c. are)
The school ............ is near our house is my school. (a. who, b. what, c. which)
1. I use my ............ to track my steps. 2. In the past people used the ............ to type letters.

Graphic organiser about 'The Sports Day': 1. How often? 2. What do you do? 3. What should each team do? 4. What does the winner get? 5. How do players feel? Guide words: yearly-warm up-race-respect-golden-proud
exercises.answer: We have a sports day every year. We warm up and run in races. Each team should race with respect. The winning team gets a golden cup. Players feel proud after winning.

teaching_notes: 4-page progress test. Reading passage about Science Museum visit tests comprehension, vocabulary in context ('effortless'), and reference words. Grammar tests both good at + verb-ing and relative clauses. Spelling tests smartwatch/typewriter recognition and -ly suffix.
